etymology.description: semantic 木(木) (mù, "tree; wood") + phonetic 登(登) (dēng) -> orange
etymology.interpretation: The character 橙 (chéng, "orange") combines 木(木) (mù, "tree; wood") as the semantic component to indicate a type of tree, with 登(登) (dēng) providing the phonetic component to suggest the sound. The meaning refers to the orange tree or its fruit.

examples.chinese: 他的衬衫是橙色的(他的襯衫是橙色的)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de chènshān shì chéngsè de.

examples.chinese: 我喜欢喝橙汁(我喜歡喝橙汁)。
examples.english: I like to drink orange juice.

examples.chinese: 橙色的花朵在花园里很显眼(橙色的花朵在花園裡很顯眼)。
examples.pinyin: Chéngsè de huāduǒ zài huāyuán lǐ hěn xiǎnyǎn.
examples.english: The orange flowers are very striking in the garden.
